Condition Summary: Three specimens in total. All looking good fresh from field and only to be mentioned; 2023.15 with former mount/wax stuck on bottom and 2023.17 has a de-attached part to be repaired.
Specimen in good condition, but grime/soil is visible in the bright impressions in calcite
A thin carbonaceous film on matrix of layered light sedimentary rock.
Fossil tree with minor stable cracking and abrasions. A sturdy specimen with handling marks/appearance. Accretions such as dirt and “finger fats”.
Display specimen with nicely cut edges. Several scratches on front of surface. Hairline break through fossil, thick repaired break left corner.

Fossil of horseshoe crab in limestone matrix. Seems like it has been consolidated previous. It´s quite dark patina gives hints of an old specimen, that might have been used for illustration in exhibition or teaching. Matrix thick and stable, fossil seems to be in excellent condition.
Fossil and shale in good condition. Residues of what is believed to be silicone on back side and edges (from moulding/casting).
Super condition. Nothing to report.
Excellent condition, but friable specimen and matrix. Thin cracking in matrix, but stable.

Flint trace fossil in good condition, with several minor breakage points and one larger especially occurring along the spiral form. One former break has been repaired with unidentified adhesive that turns a bit yellow.
Excellent condition, minor fissures on the assumed back for display. Minor areas of breakage appears to be from collecting event. One clean cut seems to be processed.
Large cylindrical coproliteical coprolite in excellent condition. Natural occurring breaks from collecting event
Large cylindrical coprolite in excellent condition. With natural occurring breakage/end.
Cylindrical coprolite with tapered end in excellent condition.
Cylindrical coprolite in excellent condition.
